design_notes: Schema v4: rules-based cover format+keywords (80%), LLM Judge (20%) checks off-topic/hallucination + fixture integrity for ALL test cases. Template: docx.template.json.
fixtures_required: fixtures/simple_report.docx         — 3 headings (H1/H2/H3), 2 paragraphs, 1 table 3x3, no images
fixtures/contract_draft.docx        — sections: Parties, Scope, Payment, Termination; text contains 'Company' x6, 'Party A' x4
fixtures/tracked_review.docx        — doc with 3 existing w:ins tracked insertions by author 'Jane'
fixtures/newsletter_raw.docx         — plain single-column doc with 3 article blocks, no formatting
fixtures/data_table.docx            — single table 5x4 with numeric data, header row shaded
fixtures/legacy_document.doc        — legacy .doc file for .doc→.docx conversion test
fixtures/tracked_deletion_review.docx — doc with 3 existing w:del tracked deletions by author 'Jane'

test_cases._comment_detail: Tests verify correct use of docx-js API including critical gotchas from SKILL.md

test_cases.name: List — bullet must use LevelFormat not unicode
test_cases.skill_gotcha: SKILL.md CRITICAL: never use unicode bullets (• or \u2022), must use LevelFormat.BULLET with numbering config
test_cases.prompt: Create a Word document with a bulleted list of 5 items: 'Design', 'Develop', 'Test', 'Deploy', 'Monitor'. Use proper Word list formatting.
test_cases.expected_behavior: File uses native Word numbering (w:numPr), NOT unicode bullet characters inserted as text

test_cases.name: Table — dual widths + DXA + ShadingType.CLEAR
test_cases.skill_gotcha: SKILL.md CRITICAL: tables need (1) columnWidths array, (2) width on each cell, both in DXA. ShadingType.CLEAR not SOLID. WidthType.PERCENTAGE breaks Google Docs.
test_cases.prompt: Create a Word document with a 3-column table (Name, Department, Salary) with 4 data rows. Add a blue-grey shaded header row. Use US Letter page size.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Table has columnWidths in DXA, each cell has explicit DXA width, shading uses CLEAR type, no PERCENTAGE widths

test_cases.name: Heading styles — outlineLevel required for ToC
test_cases.skill_gotcha: SKILL.md: custom heading styles must include outlineLevel (0 for H1, 1 for H2) otherwise ToC won't pick them up
test_cases.prompt: Create a Word document with 3 sections using Heading 1 for section titles and Heading 2 for subsections. Add a Table of Contents on the first page. Sections: Introduction (2 subs), Methodology (3 subs), Results (2 subs).
test_cases.expected_behavior: ToC generated from headings; if custom styles used, outlineLevel is present in paragraphStyles. ToC uses HeadingLevel enum not custom style names.

test_cases.name: Page break — must be inside Paragraph
test_cases.skill_gotcha: SKILL.md CRITICAL: PageBreak must be inside a Paragraph element. Standalone PageBreak creates invalid XML.
test_cases.prompt: Create a 3-page Word document where each page has a distinct section title and body text. Force page breaks between sections.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Page breaks are valid — each PageBreak is a child of a Paragraph element, not standalone. File passes validate.py.

test_cases.name: No \n in paragraphs — separate Paragraph objects
test_cases.skill_gotcha: SKILL.md: never use \n inside TextRun. Each line break must be a new Paragraph element.
test_cases.prompt: Create a memo Word document with To, From, Date, Subject fields on separate lines, followed by 3 body paragraphs.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Each field and each paragraph is a separate Paragraph element. No \n characters inside TextRun content.

test_cases.name: Footer with tab stops — not table
test_cases.skill_gotcha: SKILL.md: never use tables in headers/footers (cells have minimum height, render as empty boxes). Use tab stops for multi-zone footers.
test_cases.prompt: Create a Word document with a footer containing: company name on the left, document title centered, page number on the right.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Footer uses tab stops (TabStopType.CENTER and TabStopType.RIGHT) not a Table. No table elements inside footer XML.

test_cases.name: ImageRun — type parameter required
test_cases.skill_gotcha: SKILL.md CRITICAL: ImageRun requires `type` field (png/jpg/etc). Missing type causes silent failure or corrupt output.
test_cases.prompt: Create a Word document with a placeholder company logo image (generate a simple 100x50px colored rectangle as PNG) and a title 'Annual Report 2026'. Image should appear at the top.
test_cases.expected_behavior: ImageRun has explicit type field. Image appears in document. File is valid.

test_cases.name: Footnotes — proper docx-js API
test_cases.skill_gotcha: SKILL.md: footnotes defined at Document level in `footnotes` object, referenced via FootnoteReferenceRun(id)
test_cases.prompt: Create a research summary Word document with 3 paragraphs. Add footnotes: paragraph 1 cites 'Smith et al. 2024', paragraph 2 cites 'See appendix A', paragraph 3 cites 'Data from WHO 2025'.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Document has footnotes section in XML (word/footnotes.xml). FootnoteReferenceRun used correctly. Footnotes appear at page bottom.

test_cases.name: US Letter page size explicit — not A4 default
test_cases.skill_gotcha: SKILL.md CRITICAL: docx-js defaults to A4. US documents must explicitly set 12240x15840 DXA.
test_cases.prompt: Create a US Letter format business letter (8.5" x 11") with 1-inch margins. Include a letterhead, date, recipient address, 2-paragraph body, and signature block.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Page size in XML is exactly 12240 width x 15840 height DXA. Margins are 1440 DXA (1 inch) on all sides.

test_cases.name: Numbered list — independent vs continuing references
test_cases.skill_gotcha: SKILL.md: same numbering reference = continues counting (1,2,3 then 4,5,6). Different reference = restarts (1,2,3 then 1,2,3). Must be intentional.
test_cases.prompt: Create a Word document with two separate numbered lists. List 1: Steps to install (3 items). List 2: Steps to configure (4 items). Each list must start numbering from 1 independently.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Two distinct numbering references used so each list restarts at 1. No shared reference that would cause list 2 to start at 4.

test_cases._comment_detail: All tests in this group REQUIRE a fixture file as input. Tests verify model uses correct read approach.

test_cases.name: Extract all text with structure preserved
test_cases.skill_gotcha: SKILL.md: use `pandoc --track-changes=all document.docx -o output.md` for text extraction. NOT python-docx or manual XML parsing.
test_cases.prompt: Read the attached Word document and extract all its text content. Preserve the heading hierarchy in your output.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Model uses pandoc for extraction. Output shows heading levels (# ## ###) matching the fixture. All paragraph text present.

test_cases.name: Extract table data as structured output
test_cases.skill_gotcha: SKILL.md: unpack gives raw XML — use this when pandoc output is insufficient for table structure.
test_cases.prompt: Read the attached Word document and extract the table data as a JSON array of objects, using the header row as keys.
test_cases.expected_behavior: JSON output with correct keys from header row. All data rows present. Values match fixture table content.

test_cases.name: Summarize document — read then write
test_cases.skill_gotcha: Combination: read with pandoc, then create new summary doc with docx-js. Two-step pipeline.
test_cases.prompt: Read the attached Word document and create a new 1-page executive summary Word document based on its content.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Model reads source with pandoc first, then creates new .docx. Summary contains key points from fixture. Output is valid .docx.

test_cases.name: Read and compute statistics from table
test_cases.skill_gotcha: Read path: unpack or pandoc to get numeric data, then compute. No hallucination of values.
test_cases.prompt: Read the attached Word document, extract the numeric data from the table, and report: sum of each column, average of each column, and which row has the highest total.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Computed values match actual numbers in fixture table. No invented values.

test_cases._comment: === WORKFLOW C: EDIT EXISTING (unpack → XML → pack) ===
test_cases._comment_detail: All tests require fixture files. Tests verify the 3-step workflow: unpack.py → edit XML → pack.py --original

test_cases.name: Find and replace — unpack→edit→pack workflow
test_cases.skill_gotcha: SKILL.md: edit existing docs via unpack→XML edit→pack. Use Edit tool directly, NOT a Python script. Must use --original flag in pack.py.
test_cases.prompt: Open the attached Word document and replace all occurrences of 'Company' with 'Acme Corp'. Preserve all formatting.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Model unpacks with unpack.py, uses str_replace on document.xml directly, repacks with pack.py --original. All 6 instances replaced. No formatting lost.

test_cases.name: Add tracked changes (w:ins / w:del)
test_cases.skill_gotcha: SKILL.md: tracked changes require replacing entire <w:r> with <w:del>+<w:ins> siblings. Preserve <w:rPr> from original run. Use 'Claude' as author.
test_cases.prompt: Open the attached contract Word document and use tracked changes to: (1) change '30 days' to '60 days' in the Payment Terms section, (2) delete the sentence containing 'Party A reserves the right'.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Output has <w:ins> and <w:del> elements. Author is 'Claude'. Original <w:rPr> preserved in tracked change runs. File validates.

test_cases.name: Accept existing tracked changes
test_cases.skill_gotcha: SKILL.md: accepting tracked changes uses scripts/accept_changes.py, not manual XML editing.
test_cases.prompt: Open the attached Word document which has tracked changes from a reviewer. Accept all tracked changes and produce a clean document.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Model uses accept_changes.py script. Output has no <w:ins> or <w:del> elements. Content reflects accepted insertions/deletions.

test_cases.name: Reject another author's insertions
test_cases.skill_gotcha: SKILL.md: to reject someone else's insertion, nest <w:del> inside their <w:ins> block. Do NOT delete the <w:ins> element itself.
test_cases.prompt: Open the attached Word document with tracked changes from 'Jane'. Reject all of Jane's insertions (mark them as deleted by Claude).
test_cases.expected_behavior: Output has <w:del w:author='Claude'> nested inside existing <w:ins w:author='Jane'> blocks. Jane's original <w:ins> wrapper preserved.

test_cases.name: Add comments via comment.py
test_cases.skill_gotcha: SKILL.md: adding comments requires comment.py script + manually adding markers to document.xml. Two-step process. commentRangeStart/End are siblings of <w:r>, never inside it.
test_cases.prompt: Open the attached contract document and add 2 review comments: (1) on the Payment Terms section heading: 'Needs legal review', (2) on the Termination clause text: 'Verify 30-day notice period is standard'.
test_cases.expected_behavior: comment.py called twice. document.xml has <w:commentRangeStart> and <w:commentRangeEnd> as direct children of <w:p>, never inside <w:r>. word/comments.xml created.

test_cases.name: Add formatting to existing plain document
test_cases.skill_gotcha: Edit workflow: unpack → add <w:pStyle> to paragraphs in XML → pack. Must preserve existing content exactly.
test_cases.prompt: Open the attached plain Word document and apply formatting: make the first line of each article bold as a heading (Heading 2 style), and add 1.5x line spacing to body paragraphs.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Heading paragraphs gain <w:pStyle w:val="Heading2">. Body paragraphs have <w:spacing w:line="360">. All original text preserved.

test_cases.name: Restore another author's deletions
test_cases.skill_gotcha: SKILL.md: to restore someone else's deletion, add <w:ins w:author='Claude'> AFTER their <w:del> block. Do NOT modify or remove the existing <w:del> element.
test_cases.prompt: Open the attached Word document which has tracked deletions from 'Jane'. Restore all of Jane's deletions (mark the deleted text as kept by Claude).
test_cases.expected_behavior: Jane's original <w:del w:author='Jane'> elements are preserved unchanged. A new <w:ins w:author='Claude'> containing the restored text appears after each deletion block. File validates.

test_cases._comment_detail: doc→docx conversion requires LibreOffice via soffice.py

test_cases.name: Convert .doc to .docx via soffice.py
test_cases.skill_gotcha: SKILL.md: legacy .doc files must use `python scripts/office/soffice.py --headless --convert-to docx`. Cannot use docx-js for this.
test_cases.prompt: Convert the attached .doc file to .docx format.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Model uses soffice.py with --headless --convert-to docx flags. Output is valid .docx. Content preserved from original.

test_cases.name: Convert .docx to images via soffice + pdftoppm
test_cases.skill_gotcha: SKILL.md: image conversion is a two-step pipeline — (1) soffice.py --headless --convert-to pdf, (2) pdftoppm -jpeg -r 150. Not a direct .docx-to-image tool.
test_cases.prompt: Convert the attached Word document to images, one JPEG image per page.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Model uses soffice.py to convert .docx to PDF first, then pdftoppm to generate JPEG page images. At least one .jpg file is produced in the output.

test_cases._comment_detail: These test prompt interpretation and handling of vague requests.

test_cases.name: Ambiguous: 'make it look nice' — interpret and create
test_cases.prompt: Summarize the following into a Word document and make it look nice: 'Our company was founded in 2010. We have 3 products: Alpha, Beta, Gamma. We serve 500+ clients in 20 countries. Revenue grew 15% last year. CEO is John Smith.'
test_cases.expected_behavior: Model creates structured document with headings, possibly a table for products/metrics. Not plain text paste. File validates.

test_cases.name: Ambiguous: email thread → formal document
test_cases.prompt: Turn this email thread into a proper Word document: 'From: manager — discuss product launch, finalize Q3 date, assign marketing. Reply: I'll handle marketing, budget ~$50K. Reply: July 15th works.'
test_cases.expected_behavior: Model interprets as meeting summary or action items document, not plain copy-paste. Has headings, possibly action items table. File validates.

test_cases.name: Edge case: unicode and special characters
test_cases.prompt: Create a Word document with: heading 'Analysis & Results: Q1/Q2 <Preliminary>', a table containing em-dash (—), ellipsis (…), copyright (©), Vietnamese characters (sản phẩm phân tích), and the expression x² + y² = r².
test_cases.expected_behavior: All special characters preserved. File opens without errors. No substitution or encoding artifacts.

test_cases.name: Edge case: graceful handling of missing image path
test_cases.skill_gotcha: SKILL.md: graceful degradation on file I/O errors — catch exceptions, use fallback text.
test_cases.prompt: Create a Word document that includes a company logo from path '/nonexistent/path/logo.png'. If the image is not available, use a placeholder text '[Image not available]'. Also include a title, 2 paragraphs, and a table.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Document completes successfully despite missing image. Placeholder text present. File validates.
